accumulated_interests.interest: predictability and advance notice for planning purposes
reduction of ongoing friction or repeated negotiation
sense of order or control in a domain that feels unstable
validation that current confusion is real and needs addressing

risk_signal_history.signal: framing suggests prepared position rather than exploratory opening — may indicate Party A has predetermined outcome in mind
use of 'already causing confusion' frames the problem unilaterally without Party B's confirmation
anticipatory anxiety may be driving solution before problem is fully explored
framing 'already causing confusion' without concrete examples suggests perceived rather than demonstrated breakdown

relational_posture_progression.posture: assertive_and_organized — sets the agenda, frames the problem, proposes structural solution
assertive_and_solution_oriented — maintains agenda control but not adversarially, wants to move quickly to structure

scaffold_divergence_log.model_value: asymmetric_at_opening — Party A has established framing and problem definition; Party B's response to that framing will be the critical diagnostic for whether this is a shared concern or a contested frame. Current dynamic is unilateral rather than reciprocal.

cooperative_with_underlying_frame_tension — both parties want to work together and anchor on child welfare, but Party A is experiencing current arrangement as inadequate while Party B is experiencing it as adequate-but-improvable. This is a framing gap, not a conflict, but if not surfaced it will produce misaligned option work. Party B's 'defaults not hard rules' distinction is an attempt to preserve their reality while meeting Party A's need — this is a bridge move that should be explored.

notes: Turn 1 represents initial contact — no party input has occurred yet
No behavioral signals available to assess emotional state or relational posture
Cannot infer interests without any party communication
Pre-computed scaffold indicates 'apparently_stable' and 'engaged_and_cooperative' but this appears to be default state rather than observation-based
Perception work at this stage is necessarily prospective rather than reactive

Party A's specificity ('written defaults,' 'predictability') suggests this may be a prepared position rather than an emergent articulation of need — mediator must ensure Party B's independent perspective is surfaced before accepting this frame as shared
Party A's use of 'already causing confusion' asserts a problem state that Party B has not yet confirmed — this could be shared reality or Party A's unilateral interpretation
Party B's silence is the most important signal right now — their first response will reveal whether they share Party A's problem definition, resist it, or introduce competing concerns

Party B's turn 4 response is affiliative in tone but contains important qualifying language ('we've been doing okay', 'don't want to over-formalize') that signals their experience of the current state differs from Party A's

Party A has not yet specified what confusion has occurred or its frequency/severity — the problem remains abstract, which supports perception agent's note that they are debating structure without substance

The 'defaults vs hard rules' framing Party B introduced is load-bearing — it reveals that Party B is not resisting structure per se, but is concerned about rigidity. This distinction must be explored before option work.
Both parties used child-centered language genuinely — this is a shared value anchor that can be used to bridge frame differences

accumulated_interests.interest: preservation of flexibility and responsiveness to real-time needs
validation that current informal system has been working adequately
avoidance of over-rigid structure that feels constraining or punitive
child comfort and relational ease (explicitly named)
process legitimacy — wants their 'we've been doing okay' framing to be heard alongside Party A's concern

risk_signal_history.signal: silence after Party A's structured opening — reason unknown (may be deference, disagreement, surprise, or simply waiting for invitation)
emphasis on 'we've been doing okay' may signal feeling rushed or that problem is being overstated
could withdraw if perception grows that Party A's framing is being accepted as objective truth without Party B's reality being validated

relational_posture_progression.posture: cooperative_but_subtly_pushback — affirms collaboration while gently challenging premise that problem is urgent
